data.title: Watches of Switzerland's Pretax Profit Falls; Warns of Tariff Uncertainty
data.description:
By Adam Whittaker

Watches of Switzerland said pretax profit slipped in its fiscal 2025 despite a rise in adjusted earnings and revenue and warned that U.S tariff uncertainty made guiding for the year ahead challenging.

The U.K. luxury-watch retailer said Thursday that pretax profit for the year ended April 27 fell 18% from the year earlier to 76 million pounds ($103.6 million).

The fall came despite adjusted earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization rising 8% to 192 million pounds and revenue growing 7% to 1.65 billion pounds.

Luxury goods companies have faced high levels of uncertainty stemming from U.S. tariffs and a slowdown in global growth. Despite this, the company said revenue at its U.S. business exceeded $1 billion for the first time, while business conditions in the U.K. stabilized.

Based on the assumption that 10% U.S. tariffs are maintained, Watches of Switzerland expects constant-currency revenue growth between 6% to 10% in fiscal 2026. Its adjusted earnings before interest and taxes margin could rise by up to 100 basis points from 9.1% in fiscal 2025.

The company said the outcome of U.S. tariff developments remainrf uncertain and that it is too early to comment on the impact of future changes. The company will provide a further update on its fiscal 2026 guidance once the situation becomes clearer, it said.

Write to Adam Whittaker at adam.whittaker@wsj.com

data.title: NRW Holdings Shows Potential for More Contracts After Rio Tinto Contract Win, Says Euroz Hartleys
data.description:
NRW Holdings' new contract with Rio Tinto shows potential for more awards, including further opportunities at other Brockman sites and with Main Roads Western Australia, Euroz Hartleys said in a note Thursday.

The company on Thursday said its NRW Civil & Mining unit received a contract valued at about AU$167 million from Rio Tinto for work at its Brockman Syncline 1 mine development within the Brockman Mine Hub in the Pilbara region of Western Australia.

The research firm said it expects more awards for NRW Holdings based on the scale of ongoing opportunities in the region, highlighted by about AU$6 billion in active tendering and a broader pipeline of around AU$15 billion.

Euroz Hartleys maintained a buy rating on NRW Holdings with a price target of AU$4.23.

data.title: EQT Fund, First Kraft Raise Stake in Fortnox to 89%
data.description:
A consortium of EQT's EQT X fund and First Kraft, via Omega II, acquired an additional 13.8 million shares in Fortnox since June 27, raising ownership in the Swedish internet-based application developer to 89.2%.

The extended acceptance period for Omega II's offer to acquire all shares in Fortnox closes on July 7, according to a Thursday release. Settlement is anticipated on July 15.

After acquiring more than 90% of the share capital in Fortnox, Omega II plans to commence compulsory redemption proceedings to acquire all remaining Fortnox shares and promote its delisting from Nasdaq Stockholm.

data.title: MOIL Posts Highest-Ever June Production
data.description:
MOIL recorded its highest-ever June production of 168,000 tons of manganese ore, which was a 2% rise over the previous year, according to a Tuesday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

In the April-June quarter, the company also recorded a 7% year-on-year rise to its best-ever quarterly production of 502,000 tons. The exploratory core drilling was also higher by 16% year-on-year at 34,900 meters.

The company's shares were up nearly 1% in recent trade.

data.title: Silver Rises Ahead of Key US Payroll Report
data.description:
Silver prices climbed to around $36.80 per ounce on Thursday, edging closer to 13-year highs as persistent dollar weakness and investor caution ahead of the US jobs report drove demand for precious metals.

A softer-than-expected payroll figure could bolster expectations for a Federal Reserve rate cut as early as July.

Supporting that view, ADP data released Wednesday showed a surprise decline of 33,000 in private-sector employment for June, raising fresh concerns about labor market strength.

Meanwhile, trade developments added to the broader market narrative.

The US finalized a deal with Vietnam that includes a 20% tariff on imports—down from the originally planned 46%—raising hopes for further bilateral agreements.

data.title: Bang & Olufsen Net Loss Widens Amid Uncertain Market
data.description:
(06:01 GMT) *Bang & Olufsen 4Q 4% Revenue Growth
(06:02 GMT) *Bang & Olufsen FY25 Rev DKK2.55B
(06:02 GMT) *Analysts Saw Bang & Olufsen FY25 Revenue DKK2.50B
(06:02 GMT) *Bang & Olufsen FY25 Gross Margin Up 55%
(06:03 GMT) *Bang & Olufsen FY25 Ebit Margin Before Special Items 1%
(06:04 GMT) *Bang & Olufsen FY25 EBIT DKK26M (Before Special Items)
(06:04 GMT) *Bang & Olufsen FY25 Pretax Profit DKK2M
(06:05 GMT) *Bang & Olufsen Targets FY26 Revenue Growth 1% to 8%
(06:05 GMT) *Bang & Olufsen Targets FY26 Ebit Margin Before Special Items -3% to 1%
(06:06 GMT) *Bang & Olufsen Targets FY26 Free Cash Flow -DKK100M to DKK0M
(06:07 GMT) *Bang & Olufsen Issues FY26 View
(06:08 GMT) *Bang & Olufsen FY25 EBITDA DKK271M (Before Special Items)
(06:11 GMT) *Bang & Olufsen FY25 Net Loss DKK29M
(06:39 GMT) Bang & Olufsen Net Loss Widens Amid Uncertain Market

By Nina Kienle

Bang & Olufsen posted an expanded net loss for fiscal 2025 in what it said was an uncertain market.

The Danish consumer-electronics company on Thursday posted a net loss of 29 million kronor($4.6 million) for the year ended May 31, compared with a net loss of 17 million kronor the prior year. Revenue fell 1.6% year-on-year to 2.55 billion kronor.

The revenue figure came slightly above analysts' expectation of 2.50 billion kronor, according to consensus estimates provided by LSEG.

Earnings before interest and taxes before special items fell to 26 million kronor from 61 million kronor the prior year, which had been the highest level in six years, the company said.

The EBIT margin before special items came to 1.0% from 2.4% the prior year.

The result were in line with plans and aligned with the outlook for the year, the company said.

For fiscal 2026, the company targets revenue growth in a range of 1% to 8% and an EBIT margin before special items between negative 3% and positive 1%, it said.

"We will closely monitor tariff changes and market developments in the coming period, while staying focused on the next steps of our strategic acceleration", the company said.

Write to Nina Kienle at nina.kienle@wsj.com

data.title: Paisalo Digital Allotts Commercial Papers Worth INR300 Million
data.description:
Paisalo Digital has received board approval to allott commercial papers worth 300 Indian million rupees at a face value of 500,000 rupees each, on a private placement basis, according to a Thursday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The company allotted 600 commercial papers to State Bank of India at a yield of 11.00% with a maturity date of Sept. 4.

The commercial papers have been issued at 490,683.50 rupees each, amounting to an issue value of 294.4 million rupees.

data.title: Theta Edge Mulls Private Placement of Shares
data.description:
Theta Edge proposed a private placement of up to 23.6 million shares to third-party investors.

The shares represent 20% of the company's enlarged issued share capital, according to a Thursday Malaysian bourse filing.

The company will determine the issue price and other details at a later date, ICT service provider said.

data.title: Swedish services PMI rises to 54.6 points in June - Silf/Swedbank
data.description: Activity in Sweden's service sector increased in June with the PMI index rising to 54.6 points from a marginally upwardly revised 50.9 points the previous month, compilers Silf/Swedbank said on Thursday.

data.title: REG-Sampo plc: Disclosure Under Chapter 9 Section 10 of the Securities Market Act (BlackRock, Inc.)
data.description:
Sampo plc, stock exchange release, 3 July 2025 at 9:35 am EEST

Sampo plc: Disclosure Under Chapter 9 Section 10 of the Securities Market Act (BlackRock, Inc.)

Sampo plc (business code 0142213-3) has received a disclosure under Chapter 9, Section 5 of the Securities Markets Act. The reason for the notification by BlackRock, Inc. is the Group restructure following the acquisition of HPS Investment Partners (“HPS”). The aggregate holdings including financial instruments according to SMA 9:6a owned by BlackRock, Inc. and the entities referred to above amounts to 7.00 per cent of the total number of shares and 6.98 per cent of the total voting rights of Sampo plc.

Sampo's share capital comprises 2,691,238,860 shares, of which 2,690,238,860 are A shares and 1,000,000 are B shares. Each A share entitles its holder to one (1) vote and each B share to five (5) votes. Thus, the total number of votes is 2,695,238,860.
